touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Layla Wang introducing our AI sales platform and its productivity benefits. No response received.
Attempted cold call to Layla Wang. Call went to voicemail, left brief message about helping sales teams improve rep productivity.
Follow-up email to Layla with case study on fintech sales team improving productivity by 34%. No response.
Cold call attempt. Layla unavailable, spoke briefly with assistant who noted she's in back-to-back meetings this week.
Called Layla Wang. No answer, left voicemail mentioning our conversation intelligence capabilities.
Attempted call to Layla. Phone rang through to voicemail, left message referencing our work with similar fintech companies.
Sent LinkedIn connection request and message to Layla mentioning mutual connection in fintech space. No response yet.
Cold call attempt. Layla did not pick up, left voicemail noting this would be final outreach unless she's interested.
Breakup email sent to Layla indicating we'll stop outreach unless she wants to connect. No response.
LinkedIn message sent sharing recent article on AI in sales. Layla viewed profile but did not respond.

Qualification call with Layla Wang. She responded to breakup email. Discussed current sales tech stack and pain points around rep productivity and coaching. Team of 22 reps, currently using basic CRM with no conversation intelligence. Layla mentioned they've looked at Gong previously.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest challenge with sales team productivity right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our reps spend too much time on manual CRM updates and we have no visibility into what's actually happening on calls.

questions_asked.question: Have you evaluated any conversation intelligence tools before?
questions_asked.answer: We looked at Gong about 6 months ago but the timing wasn't right. We're revisiting now.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week
Layla to send current tech stack overview

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Layla thanking her for the qualification call and confirming discovery meeting. Sent resources on conversation intelligence ROI.
Reminder email sent to Layla about upcoming discovery call. No response needed.

Discovery call with Layla Wang. Deep dive into RidgeLens sales process, current challenges with rep onboarding, coaching, and deal visibility. Team of 22 reps across 3 managers. Average deal size $18K, sales cycle 45-60 days. Pain points include inconsistent messaging, limited coaching bandwidth, and manual note-taking reducing selling time. Layla is champion, needs to involve CRO Hiroshi and IT for security review.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently coaching your reps on calls?
questions_asked.answer: Managers try to listen to calls manually but it's inconsistent. Maybe 2-3 calls per rep per month at best.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of quarter, so next 6-8 weeks ideally.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform compare to Gong? We've been considering them as well.
questions_asked.answer: Both platforms offer conversation intelligence. We differentiate on automation capabilities and ease of use. Happy to do a detailed comparison.

next_steps: Rep to send detailed platform overview
Layla to intro CRO Hiroshi for next call
Schedule follow-up to discuss use cases

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call with Layla to discuss specific automation use cases. Covered automatic CRM logging, deal risk alerts, and coaching workflows. Layla confirmed strong interest and working on getting Hiroshi's calendar.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our current CRM system?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs. What are you using currently?

next_steps: Layla to confirm Hiroshi's availability
Rep to send integration documentation

touches.ai_summary: Call with Layla to prepare for exec meeting with Hiroshi. Discussed business case, ROI metrics, and what matters most to CRO level. Layla shared that Hiroshi cares about pipeline visibility and rep ramp time. Also mentioned IT directors Aisha and Kevin will need to be involved for security review.

questions_asked.question: What are Hiroshi's top priorities for the sales org this year?
questions_asked.answer: Accelerating new rep ramp time and improving win rates in our mid-market segment.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Standard implementation is 2-3 weeks from contract signature to full team rollout.

next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with Hiroshi
Include IT in technical eval
Prepare ROI analysis

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on technical requirements and security considerations. Layla walked through their compliance needs as a fintech company. Discussed data residency, encryption, and audit requirements.

questions_asked.question: Where is call data stored and how long is it retained?
questions_asked.answer: Data is stored in SOC2 compliant infrastructure with configurable retention policies. Default is 2 years but you control this.

questions_asked.question: Who from IT should we include in the technical evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: Aisha Sun and Kevin Hoffmann, both IT Directors. They'll need to review security and integration architecture.

next_steps: Schedule technical eval with Aisha and Kevin
Send security documentation
Prepare demo environment

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Layla with security documentation, compliance certifications, and integration guides for IT review.

Technical evaluation call with Layla, Aisha Sun, and Kevin Hoffmann. Reviewed platform architecture, security controls, API capabilities, and integration with their existing stack. IT team asked detailed questions about data encryption, SSO, and audit logging. Aisha expressed concern about implementation timeline given their current project load. Kevin asked about admin controls and user provisioning.

questions_asked.question: Do you support SAML-based SSO with our identity provider?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we support SAML 2.0 SSO with all major identity providers including Okta, Azure AD, and OneLogin.

questions_asked.question: What level of effort is required from our IT team during implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal. Primarily SSO configuration and API key generation. Our team handles the heavy lifting. Typically 4-6 hours of IT time total.

questions_asked.question: What are your data governance requirements as a fintech company?
questions_asked.answer: We need data encrypted at rest and in transit, audit logs for all data access, and ability to delete customer data on request.

objections: IT team concerned about bandwidth for implementation given current workload
next_steps: Send detailed implementation plan showing minimal IT lift
Schedule product demo with Layla and sales team sample
IT to review security docs and provide feedback

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Layla and 4 sales reps. Walked through conversation intelligence, automated CRM logging, deal risk detection, and coaching workflows. Reps were engaged and asked questions about day-to-day usage. Layla mentioned they're still comparing with Gong and will need to see pricing soon. Meeting was rescheduled once due to Layla's conflict.

questions_asked.question: How accurate is the automatic call summarization?
questions_asked.answer: Our AI achieves 95%+ accuracy on summaries. Reps can edit if needed, but most use summaries as-is.

questions_asked.question: Can managers create custom scorecards for call reviews?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable scorecards based on your sales methodology and key behaviors.

questions_asked.question: What did the team think of the platform?
questions_asked.answer: Reps loved the automation features. Biggest interest was around time savings on CRM updates.

objections: Need to understand pricing to compare against Gong properly
next_steps: Layla to gather final requirements for pricing
Schedule proposal call
Send demo recording to team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Layla with demo recording, case studies, and ROI calculator. Asked for confirmation on user count and plan tier for pricing.

questions_asked.question: Can you confirm the number of users and which plan tier you're interested in?
questions_asked.answer: 22 users total, and we'd want the unlimited automation tier to maximize productivity gains.

touches.ai_summary: Brief call with Layla checking in on demo feedback and next steps. She confirmed team is positive but wants to see formal proposal. Hiroshi is asking for competitive comparison with Gong.

questions_asked.question: Can you provide a side-by-side comparison with Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll include that in the proposal. We can also do a live comparison if helpful.

next_steps: Prepare proposal with Gong comparison
Schedule proposal review call

touches.ai_summary: Email to Layla with detailed competitive comparison document highlighting our automation advantages and implementation speed versus Gong.

Proposal review call with Layla. Presented pricing at $20K ACV for 22 seats with unlimited automation tier. Walked through ROI showing 15+ hours saved per rep per month. Layla positive on value but mentioned Hiroshi will want to negotiate and needs to be involved in final decision. Discussed implementation timeline and success metrics.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing if we commit to annual contract?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my team. What's your budget target?

questions_asked.question: What's Hiroshi's main concern at this stage?
questions_asked.answer: He wants to make sure we're getting the best value and that this will actually improve our win rates, not just save time.

objections: Pricing higher than initial budget expectations, will need to justify to exec team
next_steps: Schedule exec alignment call with Hiroshi
Prepare business case deck focused on win rate improvement
Layla to review proposal with finance

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Hiroshi Stern (CRO) and Layla. Discussed strategic value of conversation intelligence for scaling the sales org. Hiroshi focused on improving win rates and competitive positioning. Asked tough questions about ROI proof points and why not just go with market leader Gong. Discussed our differentiation on automation and ease of use. Meeting was rescheduled once due to Hiroshi's travel.

questions_asked.question: What evidence do you have that this actually improves win rates, not just productivity?
questions_asked.answer: Our customers see average 12% win rate improvement in first 6 months through better coaching and deal risk detection. Can share specific case studies.

questions_asked.question: Why shouldn't we just go with Gong since they're the market leader?
questions_asked.answer: Gong is strong, but customers choose us for faster implementation, better automation, and more intuitive interface. Your reps will actually use it daily.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Reps actually using it consistently, managers running weekly deal reviews with real data, and measurable improvement in pipeline accuracy.

questions_asked.question: What's your relationship with Gong customers? Do you typically displace them?
questions_asked.answer: About 30% of our customers previously used Gong. Main reasons for switching are easier admin, better automation, and lower total cost of ownership.

objections: Gong is the proven market leader, need strong justification to go with alternative
next_steps: Send case studies on win rate improvement
Provide detailed Gong displacement references
Hiroshi to review with leadership team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up proposal call with Layla to address Hiroshi's concerns. Discussed additional customer references and implementation support. Layla confirmed Hiroshi is leaning positive but wants to ensure team adoption.

questions_asked.question: What kind of onboarding and training do you provide to ensure adoption?
questions_asked.answer: Full onboarding program including admin training, end-user training, and 30-60-90 day check-ins with dedicated CSM.

next_steps: Schedule follow-up with Hiroshi
Send adoption playbook and training overview

touches.ai_summary: Brief exec check-in call with Hiroshi and Layla. Hiroshi confirmed he's comfortable with the platform choice but wants to negotiate on pricing before final approval.

questions_asked.question: What's the best pricing you can offer for multi-year commitment?
questions_asked.answer: Let me take that back to our team. What term length are you thinking?

next_steps: Move to negotiation phase

Get budget approval timeline from Layla

touches.ai_summary: Email to Layla confirming move to negotiation phase and requesting final decision timeline and budget constraints.

Negotiation call with Layla. Discussed pricing flexibility for annual commitment. Layla shared they have budget approved at $18K and asked if we can meet that number. Discussed contract terms, payment schedule, and renewal terms.

questions_asked.question: Can you come down to $18K for annual contract?
questions_asked.answer: That's a 10% discount. I need to check with my sales director but should be doable for annual commitment with quarterly payments.

questions_asked.question: Is $18K the hard budget constraint or is there flexibility?
questions_asked.answer: That's what finance approved. There might be small room to move but not much.

objections: Pricing needs to be at or below $18K to fit approved budget
next_steps: Get approval for $18K pricing
Send updated proposal with revised terms
Layla to confirm with Hiroshi

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment call with Hiroshi on final terms. Confirmed $18K ACV for annual contract with quarterly payments. Hiroshi asked about expansion options if they grow team and guarantees on platform performance. Discussed SLAs and success metrics.

questions_asked.question: If we add more reps mid-year, what's the pricing for additional seats?
questions_asked.answer: Pro-rated at same per-seat rate. You can add users anytime through your admin portal.

questions_asked.question: What SLAs do you provide on platform uptime?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA with credits for any downtime beyond that threshold.

questions_asked.question: Are we aligned on moving forward at $18K annual?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, assuming we can get contract finalized this week. Legal will need to review.

next_steps: Send final contract with agreed terms
Hiroshi to route to legal for review
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Email to Layla and Hiroshi with final contract reflecting $18K ACV, annual term, quarterly payments, and standard SLAs. Included redline-friendly Word version for legal review.

Final pricing alignment call with Layla to confirm contract details before legal review. Walked through payment terms, auto-renewal clause, and implementation timeline. Layla confirmed legal should complete review within 3-5 days. Discussed post-signature kickoff process.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to cancel after year one?
questions_asked.answer: 30-day written notice required before renewal date. No penalty for non-renewal after initial term.

questions_asked.question: Any concerns from your side before legal review?
questions_asked.answer: No, we're aligned. Just need legal to bless standard terms. Shouldn't be major issues.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation after signature?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off within 48 hours. I'll intro you to our implementation team as soon as contract is signed.

next_steps: Legal review in progress
Layla to update on any legal feedback
Prepare implementation kickoff
